clauses.obligation: Nonexclusive right to sublicense System and Proprietary Marks in Area to Unit Franchisees via Unit Franchise Agreements

Area defined in Schedule A. Must only deal with prospective Unit Franchisees residing within Area. Territory protection conditional on compliance — area may be reduced after 3 defaults in 12 months or 2 in 6 months
Must secure approved office within Area within 90 days. Collateral assignment of lease to franchisor required. Must have phone, computer, internet, fax, email
Mandatory 2-week initial training within 90 days. Failure to complete = franchisor may retrain or terminate and retain fee. Refresher training max once/year at franchisee expense
Loaned one copy of Anago Manuals. Cannot emulate, edit, or alter. Must provide copy to each Unit Franchisee at own cost

All client payments deposited to Anago Escrow Account (franchisor-designated). Franchisor distributes weekly less fees. Daily deposit required. Unintentional payment to wrong account = immediate default allowing franchisor to assume all billing. Franchisor has 24/7/365 access to all records including electronic/computer systems
Subfranchisor solely responsible for maintaining annual FDD updates. Failure to maintain proper updates = material breach, franchisor may terminate WITHOUT opportunity to cure
Must comply with all franchise regulations. Must retain qualified franchise attorney. Send proposed FDD to franchisor for approval. Register in applicable states. All costs borne by subfranchisor
Must use form Unit Franchise Agreement designated by franchisor. Cannot modify without written approval
Must operate in compliance with Manuals, brand standards, quality requirements. Must maintain records per franchisor's formats/schedules. Franchisor has audit rights
Minimum $50,000/year on marketing Anago services in Area. Shortfall paid to franchisor. Prorated for partial years. Must provide satisfactory proof of expenditures
Must schedule minimum number of Client Bids per month based on Area population. Failure = $150/bid deficiency fee + potential territory reduction
5% of Gross Revenues, monthly on 20th. Escalating minimums: months 1-12=$0, 13-24=$1,500/mo, then +$1,500/year. Successor agreement minimum = average of last year
2% of Gross Revenues collected, monthly on 20th
$550/month NBDS access/support, paid to franchisor
Must participate in Anago National Insurance program for entire Term. $1M GL, $2M umbrella, $1M E&O, workers' comp. Premiums paid directly to carrier

Must use marks as sole trade identification. Cannot use marks as corporate name. Must follow all identity rules. Cannot register domains with Anago marks without written consent. No unauthorized use of marks
Manuals and system information are trade secrets. All personnel with access must sign confidentiality agreements identifying franchisor as third-party beneficiary
No transfer without prior written approval. Transferee must meet standards, assume or sign current agreement, pay $10K fee. ROFR: 30 days to match, 60 days to close. Death: transfer within 6 months

25 non-curable defaults including insolvency, bankruptcy, felony, audit denial, dishonesty, unauthorized transfer, false books, repeated complaints, performance failure, 3+ defaults in 12 months. Curable defaults: 30 days to cure

Must: cease operations, de-identify, return all proprietary property, assign all Unit Franchises/Agreements and Client Accounts to franchisor, transfer phone numbers/websites/social media, cancel trade names, pay all amounts within 10 days, comply with non-compete
During term: no Competitive Business, no interference. Post-term: 24 months, within Area + other subfranchisor areas + 20 miles of perimeter. Failure to comply restarts 24-month clock

Subfranchisor indemnifies franchisor for: operations, Unit Franchise Agreement nonperformance, negligence, law violations, breach, joint employer claims, acts of subfranchisor or associated persons including Unit Franchisees

60-day good faith negotiation first. Then binding arbitration per AAA Commercial Rules, 1 arbitrator, within 50 miles of franchisor's principal place of business. Federal Arbitration Act governs. Franchisor may seek injunctive relief in court for IP matters

10-year initial term. One 10-year renewal. Renewal requires: notice 9-12 months prior, not in default, sign then-current successor agreement (may have materially different terms) + general release, meet current requirements, minimum royalty floor
All individual shareholders/members must personally guarantee all obligations under the Subfranchise Rights Agreement. Unlimited personal guaranty
If premises leased, must execute collateral assignment giving franchisor right to assume lease on termination/expiration
Must assign client accounts to franchisor on termination/expiration
Must grant security interest in collateral to franchisor

distinctive_clauses: FDD maintenance failure = non-curable termination (no cure opportunity) — Section 2.6
Unintentional client payment to wrong account = immediate default — Section 2.4(d)
Territory reduction after 3 defaults in 12 months or 2 in 6 months — Section 1.3
Non-compete clock restarts if franchisee fails to comply — Section 10.2(b)
Franchisor is third-party beneficiary of all Unit Franchise Agreements with independent enforcement rights — Section 2.5(d)
Daily deposit of all client payments required — Section 2.4(b)
24/7/365 franchisor access to all records including electronic systems — Section 2.4(e)
